info.title: TerminalFeed API
info.summary: Real-time aggregated data for AI agents. Free tier plus USDC-paid premium tier with cross-site credits on TensorFeed.
info.description: TerminalFeed serves a free public API with no auth (markets, crypto, news, weather, space, infrastructure status) and a premium tier of composed multi-source endpoints gated by bearer-token auth. Premium credits are bought with USDC on Base mainnet and are jointly redeemable on tensorfeed.ai. Free endpoints have no SLA but return stale cache on upstream failure rather than 5xx. Premium endpoints are billed atomically: credits decrement before the upstream fetch begins, so partial-success responses still count. Schema stability: new fields may be added at any time; existing documented fields, URL paths, and _meta.sources[].name identifiers will not change without 90 days of deprecation notice via /changelog and X-TerminalFeed-Deprecation response header. See https://terminalfeed.io/terms#schema-stability.

license.name: Free tier: permissive use. Premium tier: inference-only, see Terms section 17.

tags.name: free
tags.description: Free real-time data endpoints. No auth required. Standard rate limits.

tags.name: pro
tags.description: Premium composed endpoints. Bearer auth required. Pay per call in USDC credits.

tags.name: payment
tags.description: Credit purchase, confirmation, and balance check. No auth except where indicated.

agentBearer.description: Bearer token from /api/payment/confirm. Same token works on tensorfeed.ai (shared credit pool). See https://terminalfeed.io/developers/agent-payments.

error.description: Reason the request was rejected. Use this to disambiguate retry strategy.

economic.description: FRED macro indicators. Each entry has value, date (ISO yyyy-mm-dd), source.

silver.description: Omitted in v1. Will be added when a stable free upstream surfaces.

tx_hash.description: On-chain Base mainnet USDC transfer hash.

system_prompt.description: Pre-formatted multi-line string an agent pastes verbatim into LLM context. Target ~350 tokens.

PremiumPaymentRequired.description: Premium endpoint refused: missing or invalid bearer, insufficient credits, or billing service unreachable.

get.description: Compact summary of BTC, Fear & Greed, recent earthquakes, top HN story count, and ISS crew. For richer composition with predictions and history, see GET /api/pro/briefing.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: Optional comma list of tickers, e.g. AAPL,MSFT,NVDA

get.summary: FRED indicators (Fed rate, CPI, unemployment, GDP)
Status of GitHub, Cloudflare, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.

200.description: Array of trending models with id, author, name, likes, downloads, pipeline, url, updated.

get.summary: NOAA SWPC: Kp index, solar wind, X-ray flare class, active alerts
get.description: Geomagnetic Kp index, solar wind speed/density, peak X-ray flare class over last 24h (A/B/C/M/X), and up to 5 active NOAA space weather alerts. Source: services.swpc.noaa.gov. 5-minute cache.

get.summary: NASA FIRMS active fire detections (North America 24h)
get.description: Top 25 most intense satellite fire detections in North America over last 24 hours, sorted by Fire Radiative Power (MW). Includes lat/lon, approximate US state, satellite, confidence. Source: NASA FIRMS VIIRS SNPP NRT. 10-minute cache. Requires NASA_FIRMS_MAP_KEY env var on the worker.

get.summary: NWS active US severe weather alerts (top 15 by severity)
get.description: Top 15 active National Weather Service alerts ranked by severity score (severity x urgency x certainty). Tornado, flood, severe thunderstorm, hurricane, winter storm, fire, heat, wind. Source: api.weather.gov. 60-second cache.

USGS Earthquake Hazards Program summary feeds. Selectable magnitude bucket (significant, 4.5, 2.5, 1.0, all) crossed with period (hour, day, week, month). 20 selectable feeds. Returns a flattened list (id, magnitude, place, time ISO 8601, depth_km, lat, lon, tsunami, USGS detail URL). Cache TTL scales with feed window (60s for hour up to 900s for month). US Government public domain (17 USC §105). Mirrors TensorFeed's same-named endpoint for federation parity.

200.description: Earthquake feed for the selected bucket

get.description: Active National Weather Service alerts from api.weather.gov, filterable by 2-letter US state code, exact NWS event name, severity, urgency, and status. Returns id, event, severity, urgency, certainty, headline, description, areaDesc, sent/effective/expires/ends, sender_name, web URL. 60-second cache. NWS-required User-Agent header sent on every fetch. US Government public domain (17 USC §105). Mirrors TensorFeed's same-named endpoint for federation parity.

parameters.name: area
parameters.description: 2-letter US state code, e.g. CA, NY.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: Exact NWS event name, e.g. "Tornado Warning", "Heat Advisory".

get.summary: Top 20 perp futures funding rates across 4 venues
get.description: Top 20 perpetual futures contracts by absolute annualized funding rate, across Binance USD-M, Bybit linear, dYdX v4, and Hyperliquid. Includes venue, symbol, period rate, annualized %, mark price. 60-second cache.

get.summary: Open-Meteo Air Quality (US AQI, PM2.5, PM10, ozone, NO2)
get.description: Air quality snapshot for any lat/lon. Returns US AQI band, European AQI, PM2.5, PM10, ozone, nitrogen dioxide, sulphur dioxide, carbon monoxide. Per-coordinates cache (rounded to 2 decimals) for 30 minutes. Defaults to Los Angeles when lat/lon are missing or invalid. Source: Open-Meteo Air Quality API.

get.summary: Shodan InternetDB exposure scan (no key required)
get.description: Shodan InternetDB lookup. Returns open ports, known CVEs, hostnames, tags, and CPEs for public IPv4 addresses. With no parameters, returns a curated rotation across Cloudflare DNS, Google DNS, Quad9, OpenDNS, GitHub, and Fastly. With ?ip=, looks up any single public IPv4. Private and reserved ranges are rejected. 1-hour cache.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: Public IPv4 address. Omit for the curated demo set.

get.summary: Smithsonian GVP weekly volcano activity report
get.description: Latest volcano activity reports from the Smithsonian Institution Global Volcanism Program weekly bulletin. Includes volcano name, country, summary, and link. 1-hour cache.

get.summary: AI-software-stack CVE summary mirrored from tensorfeed.ai
get.description: CVE counts, exploited-in-wild flags, severity and category breakdown, and the top CVE for popular AI-stack packages (Python, ML frameworks, container runtimes, cloud CLI tools). Mirrored from tensorfeed.ai every 3 hours. Full per-CVE list is premium on tensorfeed.ai. 3-hour cache.

Current NASDAQ trade halts from the official nasdaqtrader.com feed: symbol, issue name, market, reason code (T1 news pending, LUDP volatility, etc.), halt and resumption times. Refreshed every 5 minutes. An empty list is a valid state meaning no active halts. CORS enabled, no auth required.

Ground stops, ground delay programs, airport closures, and arrival/departure delays from the FAA NAS status feed, parsed per airport with reasons. Refreshed every 5 minutes. All-empty is a valid quiet-sky state. CORS enabled, no auth required.

Latest tsunami messages from both US warning centers with a derived escalation level per entry (info, watch, advisory, warning) and an overall highest level. Refreshed every 5 minutes. CORS enabled, no auth required.

Daily US Nuclear Regulatory Commission power reactor status: fleet totals (units, at full power, reduced, offline) plus per-unit power for reduced and offline reactors. Source file is daily; cached 6 hours. CORS enabled, no auth required.
The current featured live game per Lichess TV channel (top rated, bullet, blitz, rapid, classical, chess960) with player, title, rating, and game URL. 60s cache. CORS enabled, no auth required.

Top open Kalshi events ranked by 24-hour volume with each event's leading outcome and price, from the CFTC-regulated exchange's public API. Sports parlays filtered out. Refreshed every 10 minutes. CORS enabled, no auth required.

The newest models on the OpenRouter catalog (id, release timestamp, context window, prompt price per million tokens) plus the total catalog size. Refreshed every 3 hours. CORS enabled, no auth required.

get.summary: US national debt to the penny
get.description: Latest total public debt outstanding from the US Treasury with public/intragovernmental split, 30-day and 1-year deltas, and a per-second accrual rate for live-estimating displays. Daily source data, cached 6 hours. CORS enabled, no auth required.

get.summary: Active anomaly signals across all TerminalFeed data feeds
get.description: Cron-evaluated anomaly detection: FAA ground-stop clusters, tsunami escalation, trade-halt waves, reactor outages, volcano code RED, major earthquakes and M5+ surges, VIX spikes, geomagnetic storms, extreme-weather outbreaks, provider outages, and BTC 1h volatility. Each signal carries a severity (notice, elevated, critical), a human label, the dashboard panel it points at, and a first-seen timestamp. An empty list is the normal quiet state. Refreshed every 5 minutes. CORS enabled, no auth required.

get.summary: Agentic-coding harness leaderboards (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, OpenHands, Devin, SWE-Agent)
get.description: Snapshot of public benchmark scores across SWE-bench Verified, Terminal-Bench, Aider Polyglot, and METR HCAST. Each row pairs a harness with a model, and same-model cross-harness gaps are exposed as a first-class metric. Hand-curated weekly. 12-hour cache. CORS enabled, no auth required.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: raw=full benchmark/result graph, summary=top combined leaderboard plus biggest harness gaps, gaps=full per-model harness deltas, combined=normalized cross-benchmark ranking

get.summary: Solana network health (live TPS, slot, slot time, epoch)

200.description: Live Solana network metrics: tps (most recent 60s sample), tpsAvg (3-sample average), slot, slotMs, epoch, epochProgress.

get.summary: Premium briefing: 6 upstream sources composed (1 credit)
get.description: All free /api/briefing modules plus Polymarket prediction markets, optional include filter, and optional 24-hour BTC chart series. Returns 402 Payment Required when auth fails or credits are exhausted; the credit is decremented before the upstream fetch begins, so partial-fetch responses still count.

parameters.name: history
parameters.description: When set to 24h, response includes a series.btc_24h hourly array.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/briefing \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
res = requests.get(
    "https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/briefing",
    headers={"Authorization": "Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"},
    timeout=10,
)
print(res.json())
print("credits left:", res.headers.get("X-Credits-Remaining"))

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)

get.summary: Premium macro snapshot: FRED + forex + commodities + indices (2 credits)
get.description: Composes ~14 upstream calls into one response: 7 FRED economic series, 4 USD-base forex rates, gold via PAXG, US indices SPY/DIA/QQQ, and VIX. Optional 30-day history.

parameters.name: history
parameters.description: When set, FRED entries include a series array and forex.series is populated.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl "https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/macro?history=30d" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
res = requests.get(
    "https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/macro",
    params={"history": "30d"},
    headers={"Authorization": "Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"},
    timeout=15,
)
macro = res.json()
print("Fed rate:", macro["economic"]["fed_rate"]["value"])
print("VIX:", macro["markets"]["vix"]["price"])

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)

get.description: Returns the published USDC wallet address, pricing tiers, supported payment flows (credits-first and x402 fallback), and verification metadata. No auth required. Useful as the first call in an agent's onboarding sequence.

post.summary: Quote a credit purchase, return wallet + memo
post.description: Returns the published USDC wallet, a unique memo, and a quote tying the USD amount to credits. Send USDC on Base mainnet to the wallet, then call POST /api/payment/confirm with the tx hash and memo. Proxies to the TensorFeed payment Worker; the wallet returned must match the address published at /terms section 17, /developers/agent-payments, /llms.txt, and the GitHub README.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST https://terminalfeed.io/api/payment/buy-credits \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"amount_usd": 1.00}'

x-codeSamples.label: Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
quote = requests.post(
    "https://terminalfeed.io/api/payment/buy-credits",
    json={"amount_usd": 1.00},
    timeout=10,
).json()
print("Send", quote["amount_usd"], "USDC to", quote["wallet"])
print("Memo:", quote["memo"])

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)

post.summary: Confirm USDC transfer, mint bearer token
post.description: Verifies the on-chain transaction and the memo, then returns a bearer token plus credit count. Replay-protected: the same tx hash will not credit twice.

402.description: Payment not found, expired, or replayed.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST https://terminalfeed.io/api/payment/confirm \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"tx_hash": "0xabc...", "nonce": "tf-..."}'

x-codeSamples.label: Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
resp = requests.post(
    "https://terminalfeed.io/api/payment/confirm",
    json={"tx_hash": "0xabc...", "nonce": "tf-..."},
    timeout=15,
).json()
token = resp["token"]
print("Bearer token:", token, "Credits:", resp["credits"])

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)

get.summary: Check remaining credits for a bearer token

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl https://terminalfeed.io/api/payment/balance \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
balance = requests.get(
    "https://terminalfeed.io/api/payment/balance",
    headers={"Authorization": "Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"},
    timeout=10,
).json()
print("Credits remaining:", balance["credits"])

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)

get.summary: Pre-baked function-calling tool definitions for OpenAI and Anthropic
get.description: Returns ready-to-paste tool definitions for every TerminalFeed endpoint in OpenAI function-calling format and Anthropic tool-use format. Free endpoint by design (no credits). Agent devs paste the openai or anthropic block directly into their LLM tool-use scaffold.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://terminalfeed.io/api/llm-tools?format=anthropic"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (Anthropic SDK)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests, anthropic
tools = requests.get("https://terminalfeed.io/api/llm-tools?format=anthropic").json()["anthropic"]
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
resp = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-7",
    tools=tools,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the current Fed funds rate?"}],
    max_tokens=1024,
)

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (OpenAI SDK)

get.description: Aggregates time-stamped events from 4 upstream sources into one time-sorted feed: USGS earthquakes M4.0+, Hacker News current front-page items (within the since window), recently updated Polymarket markets >= $10K 24h volume, and space launches in [-1h, +12h] window. Accepts ?since=<ISO timestamp>, defaults to 1 hour ago and clamped to the 1-hour cache horizon. Each event has type, timestamp, severity, and structured data. Saves a monitor agent from polling 4 separate upstream feeds and time-merging client-side. 2 credits ($0.04). Bearer auth. 1-hour rolling cache; sub-second response when warm.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. Returns events newer than this. Defaults to 1 hour ago. Clamped to 1 hour ago if older.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/world-deltas?since=2026-04-27T22:00:00Z" \n  -H "Authorization: Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (monitor loop)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests, time
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

TOKEN = "tf_live_<64-char-hex>"
last_poll = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=5)

while True:
    res = requests.get(
        "https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/world-deltas",
        params={"since": last_poll.isoformat()},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}"},
        timeout=15,
    )
    data = res.json()
    for ev in data["events"]:
        print(ev["type"], ev["severity"], ev["data"])
    last_poll = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    time.sleep(300)  # 5 min cadence

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (polling)

get.summary: Curated 'always start here' world-state with paste-ready system_prompt (2 credits)
get.description: The 'always start here' premium call for autonomous agents. Composes upstream sources into a curated world-state snapshot: Fed funds rate, USD-base forex (EUR/JPY/GBP/CHF), HN front page top 5, significant earthquakes 24h, upcoming space launches, top 3 Polymarket markets by 24h volume, and infrastructure status (GitHub, Cloudflare, OpenAI, Anthropic). Returns BOTH a structured JSON `context` object for parsers AND a pre-formatted `system_prompt` string (~350 tokens) the agent pastes verbatim into its LLM context window. Saves the agent from making a dozen separate calls and writing a formatter. The curation choice (which signals matter, how to compress them) is the moat. Costs 2 credits ($0.04). 5-min cache. Bearer auth required.

200.description: Curated world-state with structured context and paste-ready system_prompt.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source: curl https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/agent-context \n  -H "Authorization: Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (paste system_prompt into Claude)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests, anthropic
TOKEN = "tf_live_<64-char-hex>"
ctx = requests.get(
    "https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/agent-context",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}"},
    timeout=15,
).json()

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
resp = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-7",
    system=ctx["system_prompt"],   # paste-ready, ~350 tokens of curated world state
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Given the current macro context, what should I research?"}],
    max_tokens=1024,
)
print(resp.content[0].text)

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (paste into OpenAI)

get.description: Pre-computed Pearson correlation matrix on 30-day daily simple returns across up to 10 assets: BTC, ETH, SOL, AVAX, LINK (Coinbase candles, free), gold via PAXG-USD (Coinbase, free), and 10Y / 2Y treasury yields, trade-weighted USD index, WTI oil (FRED, requires FRED_API_KEY on the Worker). Returns both a pairs array (sorted by absolute r descending) and an NxN matrix object. Each pair tagged with relationship strength and direction. Saves the agent from fetching 10 historical price series and running covariance math. Costs 2 credits ($0.04 USDC). 30-min cache. Bearer auth required.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source: curl https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/correlation-matrix \n  -H "Authorization: Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
m = requests.get(
    "https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/correlation-matrix",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}"},
    timeout=15,
).json()
for p in m["pairs"][:5]:
    print(f"{p['a']} <-> {p['b']}: {p['pearson_r']:+.3f}")
btc_eth = m["matrix"]["BTC"]["ETH"]

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript

get.description: Self-describing index of free and premium endpoints with pricing. Recommended first call for autonomous agents.

Returns server name, MCP protocol version, supported methods, and a copy-paste claude_desktop_config.json snippet showing how to integrate the TerminalFeed MCP server with Claude Desktop using the mcp-remote stdio adapter.

post.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint implementing the Model Context Protocol over HTTP. Exposes all 21 TerminalFeed tools (9 free + 9 premium + 3 payment) to any @modelcontextprotocol/sdk client. Methods: initialize, tools/list, tools/call, notifications/initialized. Premium tools require Authorization: Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex> on the request; the bearer doubles as the MCP credential. Credits-remaining is surfaced via JSON-RPC result _meta.credits_remaining.

post.summary: Create webhook subscription (push instead of poll)
post.description: Register a webhook URL to receive push deliveries every ~5 minutes. Each fire calls the subscribed /api/pro/* endpoint, HMAC-SHA256-signs the payload using the secret returned at creation, and POSTs to your URL. Per-token cap: 5 active subs. Webhook URL must be https and not resolve to private/loopback/link-local. Each fire costs 1 credit ($0.02). Subs auto-pause on insufficient_credits.

200.description: Subscription created. Save the secret; it is returned only once.

429.description: subscription_limit_reached. Cancel an existing sub before creating another.

get.description: Returns all active and paused webhook subs owned by the bearer. Secrets are NOT returned (saved only at creation).

delete.description: Deletes the subscription. Bearer must own the sub. Subsequent cron ticks will not fire.

403.description: Forbidden: bearer does not own this subscription.

get.summary: GitHub trending repos + AI/ML focus + language mix (2 credits)
get.description: Top 30 repos created in the last 7 days sorted by stars, top 15 AI/ML-focused active repos (topic:llm + commits in last 30d), language and topic aggregates, AI/ML share of trending. Source: GitHub Search API. 30-min cache. Optional GITHUB_TOKEN Worker secret for higher upstream rate limit.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source: curl https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/github-velocity \n  -H "Authorization: Bearer tf_live_<64-char-hex>"

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
d = requests.get(
    "https://terminalfeed.io/api/pro/github-velocity",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}"},
).json()
print("AI/ML share %:", d['aggregate']['ai_ml_share_percent'])

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
